---
title: "hvtiPlotR: What's New in v2.x"
subtitle: "API Redesign · New Themes · PowerPoint Export"
author: "John Ehrlinger"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    slide-level: 2
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    embed-resources: true
    center: false             # don't vertically center — better for code slides
    smaller: true             # slightly smaller base font size
    scrollable: true          # allow long slides to scroll rather than overflow
    transition: fade          # none | fade | slide | convex | zoom
    background-transition: fade
    code-line-numbers: true   # line numbers in code blocks
    highlight-style: github   # github | monokai | dracula | a11y | etc.
    footer: "hvtiPlotR v2.3.0"  # persistent footer text
  pptx:
    # To apply your HVI template, uncomment and set the path:
    # reference-doc: graphs/RD.pptx
    slide-level: 2
  beamer:
    slide-level: 2
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: false
  
---

# The Three Key Changes

## What Changed in v2.x

::: {style="padding-left: 1.5em;"}
1. **S3 API** — single-call plot functions replaced by a two-step
   `hv_*()` constructor + `plot()` workflow

2. **Theme functions** — renamed to `theme_hv_*()`, following ggplot2's
   own `theme_bw()` / `theme_grey()` naming convention

3. **PowerPoint export** — `save_ppt()` gains `panel_box`, which anchors
   the plot panel to a fixed slide rectangle across all slides
:::

*Early v2.x dev build users: also rename the `hvti_*` prefix → `hv_*` throughout.*

# API Redesign

## The Two-Step Workflow

You now build every plot in two steps:

```r
# Step 1: construct & validate
km <- hv_survival(
  data      = my_data,
  time_col  = "time",
  event_col = "event"
)

# Optional — inspect intermediate data
km$tables$risk     # risk table data frame
km$tables$report   # summary statistics

# Step 2: render a bare ggplot, then decorate with +
plot(km) +
  scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival Probability") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

Validation and rendering are separated — inspect, subset, or re-use
`km$tables` before you ever call `plot()`.

## Before & After: Constructors

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="48%"}
**v1.x — single function call**

```r
# Everything in one call —
# no access to intermediate data
mirror_histogram(
  data         = my_data,
  score_col    = "ps",
  group_col    = "trt",
  group_labels = c("SAVR", "TAVR")
)

survival_curve(
  data      = my_data,
  time_col  = "time",
  event_col = "event"
)
```
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}
**v2.x — construct, then render**

```r
# Step 1: construct
mh <- hv_mirror_hist(
  data         = my_data,
  score_col    = "ps",
  group_col    = "trt",
  group_labels = c("SAVR", "TAVR")
)

# Step 2: render + decorate
plot(mh) + theme_hv_manuscript()

# Same pattern for survival
km <- hv_survival(my_data, "time", "event")
km$tables$risk          # inspect data
plot(km) + theme_hv_ppt_dark()
```
:::

::::

## Constructor Reference: Old → New

| v1.x Function | v2.x Constructor |
|---|---|
| `mirror_histogram()` | `hv_mirror_hist()` |
| `covariate_balance()` | `hv_balance()` |
| `stacked_histogram()` | `hv_stacked()` |
| `survival_curve()` | `hv_survival()` |
| `goodness_followup()` | `hv_followup()` |

: {tbl-colwidths="[50,50]"}

## Constructor Reference: Old → New (cont.)

| v1.x Function | v2.x Constructor |
|---|---|
| `trends_plot()` | `hv_trends()` |
| `spaghetti_plot()` | `hv_spaghetti()` |
| `longitudinal_counts_plot()` | `hv_longitudinal()` |
| `alluvial_plot()` | `hv_alluvial()` |
| `upset_plot()` | `hv_upset()` |
| `eda_plot()` | `hv_eda()` |
| *(new in v2.3.0)* | `hv_consort()` |

: {tbl-colwidths="[50,50]"}

*Early v2.x dev build users: also rename `hvti_*()` → `hv_*()` throughout.*

→ [vignette("hvtiPlotR")](https://ehrlinger.github.io/hvtiPlotR/articles/hvtiPlotR.html) · [vignette("plot-functions")](https://ehrlinger.github.io/hvtiPlotR/articles/plot-functions.html)

## New S3 Convenience Methods (v2.2)

Every `hv_data` object now speaks standard R:

```r
km <- hv_survival(my_data, "time", "event")

# summary() — prints the object header + all named tables
summary(km)

# autoplot() — ggplot2-ecosystem alias for plot()
autoplot(km) + theme_hv_manuscript()

# as.data.frame() — returns the $data slot directly
df <- as.data.frame(km)   # same as km$data
```

`autoplot()` is an alias for `plot()` — handy if you're in a `broom` or
`ggfortify` pipeline that expects the ggplot2 verb.

# Themes

## Theme API: Before & After

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="48%"}
**Old API (deprecated in v2.1)**

```r
# String dispatcher — removed
p + hv_theme("dark_ppt")
p + hv_theme("manuscript")
p + hv_theme("light_ppt")

# Keyword args — removed
p + hv_theme_dark_ppt(bold = TRUE)
p + hv_theme_light_ppt(mono_y = TRUE)
```

Deprecated aliases (`hv_theme_dark_ppt()`,
`theme_dark_ppt()`, etc.) still emit a
one-shot warning and forward to the new
function — **will be removed in v3.0.0**.
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}
**New API — v2.1+**

```r
# Named functions, ggplot2-style
p + theme_hv_ppt_dark()
p + theme_hv_ppt_light()
p + theme_hv_manuscript()
p + theme_hv_poster()

# Customize inline via ...
p + theme_hv_ppt_dark(
  axis.text       = element_text(face = "bold"),
  axis.text.y     = element_text(family = "mono"),
  legend.position = "right"
)
```
:::

::::

## Theme Customization: the `...` Pattern

The `bold`, `mono_y`, and `title_size` keyword arguments have been **removed**.
Use ggplot2's standard theme elements via `...` instead:

```r
# Old — no longer works
p + theme_hv_ppt_dark(bold = TRUE, mono_y = TRUE)

# New — pass any theme() element directly
p + theme_hv_ppt_dark(
  axis.text        = element_text(face = "bold"),
  axis.text.y      = element_text(family = "mono"),
  legend.position  = "right",
  plot.title       = element_text(size = 18)
)

# Set globally for all plots in a session
ggplot2::theme_set(theme_hv_manuscript())
```

Every `...` argument is forwarded directly to `ggplot2::theme()`,
so anything valid there works here too.

→ **Full reference:** [vignette("plot-decorators")](https://ehrlinger.github.io/hvtiPlotR/articles/plot-decorators.html)

# PowerPoint Export

## `save_ppt()` — Basics

```r
# Single plot → one slide
p <- plot(hv_survival(my_data, "time", "event")) +
  theme_hv_ppt_dark()

save_ppt(
  object       = p,
  template     = "graphs/RD.pptx",       # your HVI slide template
  powerpoint   = "output/figure1.pptx",
  slide_titles = "Kaplan–Meier: All Patients"
)

# List of plots → one slide per plot
save_ppt(
  object       = list(p_km, p_balance, p_trends),
  template     = "graphs/RD.pptx",
  powerpoint   = "output/full_deck.pptx",
  slide_titles = c("KM Curve", "Covariate Balance", "Annual Trends")
)
```

**Argument names changed from the old API:**
`plot =` → `object =`   |   `filename =` → `powerpoint =`

Every plot lands as an **editable DrawingML vector** — shapes, lines,
and text remain selectable in PowerPoint.

## `panel_box` — The Problem

`save_ppt()` places each plot at a fixed `left` / `top` / `width` / `height`
on the slide. But that positions the **outer plot boundary**, not the data panel.

When y-axis labels vary in width across slides — `"1.0"` on one slide,
`"99999.0"` on another — the wider labels consume more horizontal space and
push the panel to the right. The data area lands at a **different x-position
on every slide** (slides bounce).


## `panel_box` — The Solution

Supply `panel_box` to anchor the **panel content area** to a fixed rectangle:

```r
save_ppt(
  object       = list(p_small_labels, p_wide_labels),
  template     = "graphs/RD-dark.pptx",
  powerpoint   = "output/anchored_deck.pptx",
  slide_titles = c("Survival Curve", "Hazard Difference"),
  panel_box    = list(
    width  = 8.88,   # target panel width in inches
    height = 4.51,   # target panel height in inches
    left   = 2.58,   # leave room for the widest y-axis label
    top    = 1.29    # below the title placeholder
  )
)
```

`save_ppt()` calls `hv_ph_location()` per slide, measuring each plot's
axis chrome and adjusting the overall plot placement so the **panel lands
at the same slide coordinates on every slide**.

Develop with `theme_hv_ppt_light()` so the white panel is visible in your
IDE viewer; save into your dark template at export time.

→ **Full reference:** [vignette("plot-decorators")](https://ehrlinger.github.io/hvtiPlotR/articles/plot-decorators.html)

# Migration Checklist

## Update Your Scripts

**S3 API**

- Replace `survival_curve()`, `mirror_histogram()`, etc. with `hv_*()` + `plot()`
- If on an early v2.x dev build, rename `hvti_*()` → `hv_*()`
- Update `hv_upset()` calls: `base_annotations =` → `fill_col =`, `min_size =` → `n_intersections =`

**Themes**

- Replace `hv_theme("dark_ppt")` → `theme_hv_ppt_dark()`, and so on for each style
- Replace `bold = TRUE` → `axis.text = element_text(face = "bold")` in `...`

**PowerPoint Export**

- Update `save_ppt()` arg names: `plot =` → `object =`, `filename =` → `powerpoint =`
- Add `panel_box = list(width, height, left, top)` if your deck has variable y-axis label widths

*Deprecated aliases emit a one-shot warning — will be removed in v3.0.0.*

## Further Reading

- [ehrlinger.github.io/hvtiPlotR](https://ehrlinger.github.io/hvtiPlotR/) — package site
- [vignette("hvtiPlotR")](https://ehrlinger.github.io/hvtiPlotR/articles/hvtiPlotR.html) — SAS migration guide and constructor reference
- [vignette("plot-functions")](https://ehrlinger.github.io/hvtiPlotR/articles/plot-functions.html) — worked examples for every `hv_*()` constructor
- [vignette("plot-decorators")](https://ehrlinger.github.io/hvtiPlotR/articles/plot-decorators.html) — themes, `scale_*`, `labs()`, and `save_ppt()`

**Install / update:**

```r
remotes::install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR")
renv::snapshot()
```
